text.title: Universal Decomposition Principles
text.summary: File size limits by type, universal extraction patterns, and the LLM context benefit of decomposition.

rule.statement: Cap files at these line limits: Model/Entity 150 max (target 50-80), Service 200 max (target 80-120), Controller/View 150 max (target 60-80), Component 150 max (target 50-80), Hook/Composable 100 max (target 40-60), Test 300 max (target 100-150).
rule.rationale: A single Read of a 200-line service fits in one LLM context window without truncation. Fat controllers at 500+ lines require multiple Reads and force the agent to maintain state across chunks, degrading task success rate from ~95% to ~60%.

section.title: LLM Context Benefit
section.p: Fat controller/model (50K+ tokens required): ~60% LLM task success rate.
Service layer extracted (20-30K tokens): ~85% success rate.

rule.statement: Before running an LLM-assisted feature on a module, check if any file in the touch path exceeds 200 lines. If yes, extract first, then run the feature.
rule.rationale: The decomposition investment pays back immediately on the first LLM task. Running the feature first on a fat file embeds the debt deeper as new code is layered on top.

section.title: Universal Patterns
section.p: Service Layer: extract business logic from controllers. Services own writes and side effects.
Repository/Selector: abstract data access. Selectors own reads — pure, no writes, no side effects. Enables cache-invalidation reasoning by agents.
DTO/Input Objects: explicit data transfer contracts. Make them the contract the LLM reads first before reasoning about a feature.
Composition: small components assembled into larger ones. Each component has one job.
Hooks/Composables: reusable stateful logic extracted from components. Only extract when used in 2+ components OR when the hook exceeds 30 LOC of stateful logic.

rule.statement: Make one commit per extraction (one service, one selector, one hook). Never combine "extract service" + "rename" in one diff.
rule.rationale: Agents lose blame trail when extractions are combined. Reviewers (and future agents) cannot identify which change introduced a regression. Single-extraction commits enable surgical revert.
